seoTitle: How to Create a Folder in Squarespace 7.1 Easily
metaDescription: Create folders in Squarespace 7.1 to group blog posts, images, or product listings and keep your site's navigation clean and organized.
intro: As a Squarespace site grows, keeping pages, posts, and listings organized becomes essential for both navigation and presentation. This guide shows how to create a folder in Squarespace 7.1 to group related content together, from blog posts to product listings. It's a simple step that pays off in a more professional, easier-to-navigate site.

steps.heading: Access the folder feature
steps.body: Open the site navigation area in Squarespace 7.1 where the folder option is available.

steps.heading: Create and organize your folder
steps.body: Set up a new folder and group related pages or content inside it, such as blog posts, images, or product listings.

steps.heading: Optimize your navigation structure
steps.body: Arrange folders and pages so visitors can find related content easily, improving overall site usability.

steps.heading: Avoid common mistakes
steps.body: Watch out for over-nesting folders or grouping unrelated content together, which can confuse visitors instead of helping them.

steps.heading: Review your organized site
steps.body: Check your navigation menu to confirm the new folder structure makes sense and improves the flow of your site.

faq.question: What's the benefit of using folders in Squarespace 7.1?
faq.answer: Folders help group related blog posts, images, or product listings together, making your site's navigation cleaner and more professional.

faq.question: Can I nest folders inside other folders?
faq.answer: Squarespace 7.1 supports organizing content into folders, though it's best to avoid overly deep nesting that could confuse visitors.

faq.question: Will creating a folder affect my existing pages?
faq.answer: Existing pages can be grouped into a new folder without losing their content, since the folder simply changes how they're organized in navigation.

faq.question: Is this feature only available in Squarespace 7.1?
faq.answer: This tutorial specifically covers the folder feature as it works in Squarespace 7.1.
